Recent coverage highlights Comcast's Q4 2025 struggles, including 1% revenue growth, 10% EBITDA decline, broadband subscriber losses, and widening Peacock losses amid heavy NBA costs[2][3][4]. Positive notes include an attractive 4.45% dividend yield with 18 years of growth, strong free cash flow, and a low valuation suggesting limited downside[4][5]. DNOW Inc. reported Q4 2025 results on February 20, 2026, with revenue rising to $959 million but a GAAP net loss of $147 million due to merger and inventory charges, compared to prior-year earnings. Full-year revenue grew to $2.82 billion, though GAAP net loss was $89 million versus prior profit. The stock plunged 18-19% to around $13.20 amid the earnings reaction.
